name: Frustrated Daily Commuter
one_liner: A daily commuter who relies on the MTR app for quick journey info and ticket purchases but is plagued by bugs, poor UX, and aggressive prompts.

claims.claim: Quickly check train schedules and arrival times with minimal effort.
Easily buy and manage tickets or passes (e.g., 都會票) within the app.
Redeem rewards (e.g., free rides) without issues.
Wants a reliable and fast way to plan journeys and avoid delays.
Hopes to earn and use points/积分 from travel or games for small rewards.
Prefers a simple, intuitive interface like the old version.
App is slow, hangs, or crashes frequently, especially during key tasks like viewing maps or redeeming rewards.
Endless pop-ups for features and ratings interrupt the core function of checking arrival times.
Poor UX in route selection: cannot easily change just departure or just arrival; always resets both.
Game/Fun分 section is buggy: swiping right exits the game screen and consumes tickets without reward.
Redemption process is broken: coupons disappear, shops cannot scan, tickets cause app crash.
Prefers using the mobile app for transit info, but wants the old simpler design back.
Calls customer service hotline when app fails, but often gets no real fix.
Uninstalls the app in frustration after repeated bugs (e.g., game ticket loss, crash).
Leaves 1-star reviews as retaliation for forced rating pop-ups.
Tries to use the app for quick glances (e.g., schedule, map) but is blocked by slow loading or intrusive prompts.

representative_quotes.text_original: The more they update the poorer the app is. Pretending that the updates are fancier more helpful but it totally isnt the case... Such a simple function - trying to check how long the journey from point A to B is. And they couldnt even show the eta duration.

data_source_coverage.bias_warning: 77% of evidence from a single source (app_store_hk); no social/video_comments coverage
